pages.text:
who is now verger. I knelt on a primrose. Bell ringers. A very welcoming church. The woman vicar (who had a 60th birthday tea yesterday) was away & the service was taken by a prison chaplain. Cherryard. Good sermon on doubting Thomas.
    No pub open at Prees so we drove on to Hadnall, Saracens. Best pub lunch ever in well-decorated annex.
    Off home in hot sun to plant the plants.
__Friday May 24th__
    7 Simon, Eve & Tonic left for __St. David's__. Eve has a new chair seat & Tonic has a new perch. We had a pretty drive west through showers & sunshine. We reached Hundred House**** west of __Kington__ west of Leominster at 9.10. Cold wind. Simon hurt his ribs coming back through Westfield Rd. window after inspecting a roof bar. Eve slept badly worrying about refugees in Ladywood.

Saturday May 25th. Peter's birthday in Tibet.
	Campsite surrounded by hawthorne hedges being laid. Campsite owner enters hedging competitions. He gave us advice on laying out hedge - cut 2/3 through in spring when sap rising.

We drove on to Pembroke Castle. It has the same layout as Sudley + Ludlow. The most amazing part of it is Wogan's Cavern, where supplies from boats were unloaded + carried up a spiral staircase into the castle. It is huge + was lived in for 1000 of years.
	We drove on to Solva, but rainsqualls were so heavy that we didn't get out of the van. We reached the campsite. Rhosson Gand ***** as good a crew as the Normandy campsite, but no lighthouse. Absolutely fabulous seascape. Heavy rain crashing waves.  __St. David's__

__Sunday May 26th__
	Overcast, but not raining. It was good to get out of the van. No sheep + we're the only people. So Tonic off the lead. We cycled off to the cathedral for an 11.15. Eucharist.
	The people are pretty short + the pews were too close together. Trinity Sunday. We

are called to worship God, not to understand God. No boys in the choir - girls + men. Small but very good singing. Marvellous arch decorations. Misercords. Shrine of St. David's. Simon bought a green knapsack.

After church the sun shone + we have had continuous hot sunshine all afternoon. We sunbathed, then walked the coastal path. We saw the life boat. An 18 year old became the youngest lifeboat captain after rescuing 5 men. We walked past the golf course to see Joan [?Tillett]'s campsite. The icecreams were delicious. Simon had coffee. I had toffee. No sheep, so Tonic has been off the lead. Lovely wild flowers.
	Simon had a shower. We ate in the van. Rain in the night.
	__Monday May 27__
	It remained overcast until 4 pm. We drove to __Solva__ + parked on the quay. There were young people learning to canoe. The village is pretty but a bit too pretty. A footbridge was opened a hundred years ago by the M.P.

We walked up to the Iron Age camp s.e. on a hill. Magnificent. A sloping valley next to the hill had water running through it, but we could not understand why the floor of the valley sloped, whereas on the other side of the hill it was an estuary.*
   We had a good lunch at the pub. We drove back to the campsite + the sun came out. We walked down to the life boat + then left along the cliff. The wild flowers are pretty. Sheep. Water fall out of the cliff. We turned inland, past a farm for city children, through a swamp + back to the campsite just as it got cold.
   * We also visited "the Pumpkin Shed", a marvellous walled organic garden, farmed by a Dutch woman. Plant garlic with roses to prevent black spot, because of sulphur given off by garlic. Simon liked the red clover. Crimson. We bought 2 cotton lavender, not real lavender. To prevent moths. Upside down bottles as a pavement.
   There was long heavy rain in the night.

__Tuesday May 28th__
   It stopped raining as we woke up. We drove into St. Davids + looked at the Cathedral, Holy Trinity Chapel + miserichords again. Then we saw a craft fair. Simon bought a vase.
   Eve bought a [?wax] woven bookmark + sea gull brooch. Simon liked etching by David Beattie, particularly "Market Shadows" so we set off for his workshop the other side of Fishguard to the north. Mr. Cardigan.
   He framed the etching in a lighter colour wood while we had lunch in the van in his village, __Llandygwydd__. The church has been taken down + only the font + graveyard remains. The congregation now meet in the old, old church, which is tiny.
   We set off for home, via Tregaron, over very high hills where we have stopped for tea s. of Elan valley. Huge views, but it's raining. Driving to Beulah (24 mi)
   Arrived at Cleobury Mortimer + had fish + chips. Then we called in at Trumpley to see Jim + Margaret + Helen, on our way home.
